entry.title: Can not use the SAP Gateway Server (ES5) in the Learning 'Developing with SAP Integration Suite'
entry.content:
Hi everyone,
I would like to ask for advice regarding Integration Suite.
"I am currently taking the SAP Learning Journey: 'Developing with SAP Integration Suite' using an SAP BTP Trial account.

While following the exercise 'Log on SAP Gateway Demo Server - ES5' within the unit 'Exploring the Capabilities of SAP Integration Suite', I noticed that the ES5 demo server has been decommissioned, as mentioned in this tutorial: https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/gateway-demo-signup.html

This has stalled my progress. To continue with the subsequent exercises as intended, what would be the best alternative.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: IH08 selection execution possible only by clicking valuation button
entry.content:
Hello,

After upgrade of the system we can't execute some of our variants in IH08 and IE05.

We give the equipment description and the class. But system does not return any data:

Only if we go to valuation and click it there it works

Do you know why?

entry.title: SAP SuccessFactors: Build your Adoption Program​ to Maximize Results (English; APAC)
entry.content:
PLEASE NOTE: Selecting the ‘Will you be attending?’ option will NOT register you for the event.

To register for this event, please click here: Register Here

SAP SuccessFactors: Build your Adoption Program​ to Maximize Results (English; APAC)

This session will help you understand the relevant components of an adequate adoption program to maximize your cloud investment and ensure sustained business value. We will walk you through the essentials to orchestrate Change Management, Governance, Sustainment, and Value Management and how they work together to drive ongoing adoption, solution optimization and user experience.​ Participants will explore best practices and take away actionable templates to build an initial transformation roadmap, define clear roles and responsibilities, and identify the right executive sponsors and stakeholder groups.​ This webcast is a foundational session that will lead into optional follow‑on topic specific webcasts on your preferred topics.. At the end, you will leave with a concrete structure and toolkit to start or refine your own SuccessFactors adoption program.

Please contact SAP_SuccessFactors_Onboarding@sap.com for any questions on this event.

Here is a link to our Global schedule

entry.title: Get BP API is not giving complete payload
entry.content: I have created BP and using Get BP API in postman, this is not giving complete payload.  It is only giving first and last name.

entry.title: Re: Replication Flow not being selected as Analytic model...
entry.content:
Hello @NicolasRivas1991 ,

The short answer to your question is: Sorry, there is no way. 

Why is this happening?

In Datasphere, you can download the CSN / JSON file for almost any object. 
For the Analytical Models. that file contains the full dependency tree for that object, down to local / remote tables, including all associations. 

When you're creating a package, Datasphere unravels the CSN file to find all the dependencies. 

For a replication flow, the CSN file contains the connection, the source and the target.

So, to answer your question: It happens because the way SAP decided to build the CSN files dosen't include the replication flows. 
Why did they make this decision? God knows...🤷‍♂️

It happens with every asset?

Same as above - the replication flow isn't stored as a dependency. 

How can I achieve this?

With great difficulty, I'm afraid. 
When you add a replication flow to a package, all the target tables are added.

A solution, while low-tech as all hell, is to keep all your replication flows in a folder (Maybe called Dependency flows) and add them to your package. 

Sorry I can't be more helpful 🙂

entry.title: Reference architectures for developing custom AI solutions in SAP BTP
entry.content:
AI is powerful.  Architecture makes it scalable.

In our live session, “Reference architectures for developing custom AI solutions in SAP Business Technology Platform,” we’ll cut through the noise and focus on what matters:

  • AI capabilities across SAP
  • SAP Business AI and Joule in action
  • Custom AI development options
  • Proven reference architectures and use cases

The thing is, customers don’t just want AI features.  They want outcomes, automated processes, smarter decisions, measurable impact.  And that starts with the right foundation in SAP BTP.

If you’re driving sales, shaping solutions, or delivering implementations, this session will sharpen your AI playbook.

Let’s move from experimentation to enterprise-grade execution.

Register now.  https://partneredge.sap.com/en/library/education/psd/2026/jan/e_oe_te_w_PSD_WEB_00012763.html

#SAP #SAPBTP #SAPBusinessAI #Joule #SAPEnablement #AIInnovation

entry.title: Change of declarant for VAT reporting
entry.content:
Hello,

We need to change the declarant details, such as name and ID, for VAT reporting generated from app Run Statutory Reports - the system is S4/HANA Public Cloud.

I know that this should be done via CBC, but I can't find the exact configuration item.

I'll much appreciate to get a short step-by-step configuration guide.

Thank you in advance!

 

Regards,

Plamen

Shelly Group

entry.title: Re: WebI - Calculate the sum of daily average values acro...
entry.content: "Aufgeteilt in %" is a variable simple calculation. However, we found a workaround. We excluded the Soll Stunden from the original query. Created a seperated universe that gets the Soll-Stunden completly seperated and merge them in the WebI document. Then BO aggregates the CATS Hours and joins the Soll-Stunden after aggregation and we got the single value.

entry.title: Change company code in f110 proposal
entry.content: Change company code in f110 proposal stage in AFS ecc6 based on some logic for only one company code

entry.title: Re: Inconsistent Production Version Determination Between...
entry.content:
1. Best practice is you explicitly assign the same production version in both MRP 4 view and Costing View 1 of the material master.
  2. If you need more advanced control (e.g., multiple versions for different plants or scenarios), configure Quantity Structure Control to enforce consistent selection.
  3. Avoid leaving production version determination to “automatic/random” logic — that’s what causes variances.

entry.title: Leavers process
entry.content:
Hi,

I have just started a new position and we use SAP payroll, I would like to check if there is a workflow that allows HR to mark a leaver then it flows to payroll and it calculates any over/under taken holiday as I have been told it does not do this and I find that hard to believe as a basic task of payroll.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: CFOs seeking AI – Optimal decisions using SAP IBP in Product Making (manufacturing)
entry.content: I work in procurement, but I find your report and your views very interesting. Thank you very much for that. Frank

entry.title: Update and add of conditions price MM in Purchase orders in mass
entry.content:
Dear support, 

We would like to update and add conditions price MM of purchase orders items in mass, not manually with this button 
Is there an application or program that allows mass Update of the Conditions tab at the purchase order level?

The customer would like to update the conditions of purchase order items with the value “C”  
If there is no standard application available, could you please provide a workaround (API, BO, job, etc.)?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Update and add of conditions price MM in Purchase ord...
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there is currently no standard application or background job available to mass update the Conditions tab at the Purchase Order item level or to trigger pricing redetermination with value “C” in bulk. The “Update” function in the Conditions tab is designed for manual processing per individual PO item.

As a workaround, SAP provides the released OData service API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV, which can be used to update Purchase Order items programmatically. Using this API, it is possible to trigger pricing redetermination (pricing type “C”) and update or add conditions in mass through an external integration, middleware, or automated job.

Direct table updates, batch input programs, or custom ABAP developments are not permitted in Public Cloud. Therefore, the supported and upgrade-safe approach is to use the released Purchase Order API for mass processing.

Thanks and regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Data Cloud Certificate
entry.content:
Hi @p_m8
Please check our What are the costs to get and stay certified?
Access to expert-led live sessions requires a valid SAP Learning Hub subscription.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: C_IEE2E Practical Exam Under Review → Attempt Closed...
entry.content:
Hi @Taofiksanni 

Thank you for your inquiry.

Please note that this assessment type requires a system‑based evaluation, which may occasionally result in a short delay before the outcome is displayed. In such cases, the result should appear within a maximum of 15 minutes. After that time, the learner can always view the final result under My Learning → Certification.

As of Friday, we also display the individual scores for all system‑based assessments. This allows you to see how close you were to the required cut score. Please be aware that this enhancement applies only to assessment results generated from Friday onward.

In the current case, we kindly ask you to initiate a second attempt. Once completed, the achieved score will be visible under My Learning → Certification.

If further clarification is needed, I will be glad to assist.

Kind regards

Margit

entry.title: Re: Using the EXTENSIONIN parameter of BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CR...
entry.content:
To fill structures BAPE_SDBILLINGDOC and BAPE_SDBILLINGDOCITEM in the EXTENSIONIN table parameter:

(Custom Fields and Logic in the BAPI extension)

  • Pass the structure names (constants)
  • Fill the value part (generate xml string) with class CL_CFD_BAPI_MAPPING interface IF_CFD_BAPI_MAPPING method IF_CFD_BAPI_MAPPING~MAP_TO_BAPIPAREX_SINGLE or IF_CFD_BAPI_MAPPING~MAP_TO_BAPIPAREX_MULTI (use method GET_INSTANCE and not CREATE statement)

Ask your admin for correct documentation of parameter, missing translation or dirty note implementation  ?

entry.title: Re: RSH unable to assign people on board app- resolved!
entry.content:
For anyone else facing the same issue.

If the configuration is on place, check that the catalogs are setup correctly;
Transaction /n/UI2/flpca search for role and the app and check that the Service Activation Status Text is shown as All services are active for the right APP.

Check APP /UI2/FLPCM_CUST for the catalogs; SAP_RSH_BC_MPLAN_OV and SAP_EAM_BC_PLNBKTS_MNG

Check Services and that there are no Service Activation Statuses inactive.

entry.title: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
As a Developer Advocate, I have the privilege of working with some amazing colleagues in that team, but also in the wider Developer and Community Relations organisation. For the size of the community, it's a small group, and the folks are working hard to keep the wheels on the bus and rolling in the right direction. But they can only do so much. In this post, I want to highlight a challenge for us all, and how we can, again, collectively, tackle it together.

The challenge

I'm talking about content quality, which to a greater or lesser degree has for a long time been a topic of debate both in the questions area and also the blog post area. My relationship with the SAP Community platform goes back to day 1, with SDN.

Actually, it goes back to well before that - creating and running the first online SAP community in 1995, and co-creating the SAP Developer Network has caused me to never stop caring for and worrying about those platforms, the people, and the content.

Blog post quality

The quality of some blog posts on the SAP Community platform could be better. I know it. You know it. We all know it. The number of people like you and me who worry about this sort of thing is far outweighed by the larger number of folks that make up the entire community.

Over time various folks have attempted to help educate folks so that they create better content. Here are some posts from me on this topic:

  • Help us to help you - share your code (2014)
  • Help us to help you - good questions beget good answers (2019)
  • Monday morning thoughts: a good blog post (2018)
  • Monday morning thoughts: on starting blogging (2018)

These attempts have worked, but only to a certain extent. Not only that, but we have AI content generation to contend with too. While used appropriately, and with thought, this approach can produce great results. But it can also produce poor content, especially when the human component in this equation is not entirely engaged with the purpose and process. Moreover, the ease with which poor content can be produced is astounding. And so the problem has got worse over time, and I can only see that progression accelerating.

The SAP Community belongs to us

Like you, seeing blog posts that don't belong makes me sad and frustrated. So, what should we do in this situation?

Report important issues to the moderators

Well, first, there's a decision to be made. Does the blog post break the important parts of the Rules Of Engagement? What are the important parts? Well, a bit of common sense will help. Does it defame, injure or harass? Is the content illegal? Report it to the moderators.

Feel empowered to call other issues out yourself

In other cases, don't report it. Use your voice publicly, by attaching a comment to the post, explaining briefly what you consider inappropriate, or just plain incorrect. Encourage your fellow SAP Community members to upvote your comment if they agree.

Use the power of the collective, the power of the shared heart, to highlight, educate, and yes, if you want to think of it in this way, to shame the author into adjusting or removing the post and changing their ways.

This is how society works. And the SAP Community platform is an online society. So feel empowered to treat it as one. Call the person out. Gently, politely, firmly, however you see fit. You be the judge - we're all adults here. Don't treat it as a kindergarten and go to the teacher whenever you dislike something.

Why? Because the teachers are in short supply. The moderators behind the scenes are working flat out on an ever increasing load of reports. Some that I would definitely class as important, but many, many others that I'd class as trivial and inappropriate.

Celebrate good posts

Calling out bad posts like this is important. Upvoting such call-out comments is also important.

But as well as calling out bad posts, remember to celebrate good ones by adding comments of praise or encouragement. Or just upvote them. That is the least you can do, it only takes a second.

The power of filtering and natural selection

Calling out poor content by leaving a comment helps educate, even gently admonish, if needed. But upvoting and adding multiple positive comments helps even more, not only to highlight good content, but also to feed the filter mechanisms that can surface content that you want to see, controlled in part by you.

Final thoughts

Thanks for reading this far. Here's a TL;DR (yes, I know it should go at the top, but think of it as the call to action instead).

Stop reporting poor quality content, and similar transgressions. Call the authors out yourself. Everyone is a member, and everyone is a moderator. And if we moderate ourselves with respect, honesty and firmness, we just might turn this giant sea-going cargo ship towards a better quality future for the community platform that belongs to all of us.

Originally published on qmacro.org

entry.title: Treasury Related Learning Journey & Certifications are gone missing
entry.content:
Dear Treasury experts,

I have agreed with my employer that my development path is to learn about SAP Treasury and get Certified. 

Here was the Treasury Certification  : https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-associate-treasury-with-sap-s-4hana

Here was the Treasury Learning Journey: https://learning-journeys-prod.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/#/learning-journeys/learningJourney/500333db7a2610148b2b97686142a8b8

Both links no longer work...

Anyone knows what is going on? were they replaced with some other Learning Journeys & certification routes? 

Or what shall i do if i want to learn the subject properly and structurally?

Cheers,

 

Daria

entry.title: Re: Single Freight Order with Multiple stages
entry.content: With that approach basically I'm getting 2 FOs. One from A to B, another from B to C. (My case is only A-B-C). I believed I tried everything and still dont get why it is creating 2 FOs instead of one...

entry.title: Designing a Multi‑Tenant Solutions on SAP BTP – Architecture and Prototype Walkthrough
entry.content:
Introduction

Last year, I got an opportunity to work on a project where we had to build  multi-tenant APIs on SAP BTP.  The real-life constraints were the interesting part: multiple customers, strict data isolation, centralized lifecycle management, and the need to keep operations as simple as possible. This experience inspired me to learn more about multi-tenancy, build a standalone prototype and understand the design. I created a full stack application that demonstrates multi-tenancy with CAP, MTX sidecar, SaaS provisioning and tenant-specific persistence. In this blog, I will walk you through the core concepts, the architecture and the concrete steps to get you started.

What is multi-tenancy?

Before touching any code, it’s important to understand what multi-tenancy actually means in the SAP BTP context. In simple terms, multi-tenancy means that a single running instance of your application serves multiple customers (tenants), while keeping their data strictly isolated. The below diagram gives a simple representation of how multi-tenant applications are different from single tenant applications

 Figure-1

 We usually talk about two perspectives:

  1. Provider: The global account owner who develops and operates the SaaS solution.​

  2. Subscriber: The customer (or department) that subscribes to the provider’s application and has its own users and identity provider.

In a multitenant architecture:

  1. One deployed app and shared platform resources serve many subscribers, which optimizes compute usage.​

  2. Tenant-specific data is stored in separate schemas or containers so that each tenant only sees its own data.​

  3. The platform (via services like XSUAA, Service Manager, SaaS Registry, and CAP’s MTX) helps route requests and resolve the correct tenant database at runtime.

In the prototype, I have created a full stack application (following standard CAP layout), but the same principles apply to any real-world business scenario.

How to achieve it on SAP BTP?

​In the prototype, multi-tenancy is achieved by combining - CAP, the MTX microservice and several BTP services such as XSUAA, Service Manager and SaaS Registry. Conceptually, there are three key stages: provider deployment, subscriber onboarding and tenant-specific runtime request handling.

1. Provider subaccount – deployment and setup

On the provider subaccount, you deploy your CAP application along with the multi-tenancy infrastructure. The below diagram show cases the provider subaccount perspective

Figure-2

  1. The CAP application (srv + UI) and the App Router are deployed as Cloud Foundry apps from an MTA archive.​

  2. Cloud Foundry deploy also provisions the XSUAA instance (tenant mode - shared) and a Service Manager instance to orchestrate tenant-specific database provisioning.​

  3. An MTX sidecar microservice is deployed as a separate CF app to handle subscription, onboarding and off-boarding of tenants.​

  4. A SaaS registry instance is configured with callback URLs pointing to the MTX sidecar’s endpoints (for example: onSubscription and getDependencies).​

  5. A technical tenant (often referred to as - t0) is prepared to store tenant registry and runtime metadata.

This setup turns the CAP project into a SaaS-ready multi-tenant application on BTP. 

2. Subscriber subaccount – subscription and tenant provisioning

When a customer subscribes to the application from their own subaccount, the platform and MTX collaborate to create tenant-specific resources. The below diagram show cases the subscriber subaccount perspective

Figure-3 

  1. The subscriber initiates a subscription to the provider’s SaaS app from the Service Marketplace.​

  2. SaaS Registry calls the MTX sidecar callbacks: first getDependency (if any dependent services are needed), then onSubscription to start onboarding.​

  3. MTX registers the tenant in t0 and then triggers Service Manager to provision the tenant’s database artifacts (for example, a dedicated HDI container/schema and binding).​

  4. The CAP model is deployed into this new tenant schema using the deployment and model provider services.​

  5. Finally, a tenant-specific URL is generated and stored so that the subscriber can access the app via their own hostname.

Note: An important operational step is mapping the subscriber hostname as an additional route to the provider’s App Router in the provider space, so that tenant-specific URLs resolve correctly. This is outlined in the GitHub repository as well.

3. Runtime – What happens when a user opens the tenant URL

Once subscriptions are in place, the multi-tenant behavior becomes visible at runtime. The below diagram show cases the subscriber subaccount perspective

Figure-4

  1. A user accesses the subscriber-specific URL which is routed to the provider’s App Router.​

  2. The App Router inspects the host/subdomain, derives the tenant identifier and initiates authentication with the shared XSUAA instance.​

  3. XSUAA delegates authentication to the subscriber’s IdP (for example, IAS or a corporate IdP) and returns an authorization code to the App Router.​

  4. The App Router exchanges this code for a JWT access token and forwards requests to the CAP backend with that token.​

  5. CAP resolves the tenant from the JWT and looks up the corresponding tenant-specific DB binding via MTX and Service Manager.

  6. Application executes the request against that tenant’s schema or HDI container and data is presented to the user.

From the user’s perspective, they simply use the subscriber specific URL and see the data, while the provider operates a single shared application landscape.

Git repository

The full prototype which includes the CAP project, MTX sidecar configuration and step-by-step instructions is available in the GitHub repository The repository contains:​

  1. A standard CAP layout project created using Joule For Developers.​

  2. Multi-tenancy configuration in package.json, mta.yaml, xs-security.json and xs-app.json.​

  3. The mtx-sidecar microservice used to manage tenant lifecycle operations.​

  4. Detailed README with architecture diagrams, local testing and BTP deployment steps.

You can clone the repository, enhance the domain model and adapt the setup to your own multi-tenant scenarios on SAP BTP.

Conclusion

Designing and operating a multi-tenant SaaS application on SAP BTP can look complex at first, but CAP and MTX abstract much of the heavy lifting behind a clean programming model and well-integrated platform services.

By combining CAP’s multi-tenancy features with XSUAA, Service Manager, SaaS Registry and Cloud Foundry, you can run a single codebase that securely serves multiple customers with isolated data. The prototype shows the journey from local testing (with mocked tenants) to a full BTP setup with provider and subscriber accounts, subscriptions and tenant-specific URLs. 

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my real world experience and learnings.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/get-started/
  2. https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/multitenancy/#enable-multitenancy
  3. https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/sap-business-technology-platform/developing-multitenant-applications-in-cloud-foundry-environment

entry.title: Using a Lookup Table for Profit Center Determination in Service Contracts
entry.content:
With SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 we enabled a couple of new fields to be used for profit center determination in service contracts: 

  • Bill-to Party 
  • Sold-to Party 
  • Ship-to Party 

Using these new fields can lead to a lot of manual input during period-end closing, because a lot of values of these new fields determine the same profit center. To reduce the effort, I recommend using a lookup table, which is easier to maintain than maintaining every single customer you have. So, here is a guide on how to effectively use a table lookup in the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app to determine profit centers for service contracts. 

Before we can get down to the nitty-gritty, let’s first clarify some of the prerequisites and terminology involved. 

Prerequisites 

Before you implement the Profit Center Derivation, you need to check the profit center determination access sequence described in the following link for the Help Portal page: 
Profit Center Determination for  Service Transactions 

Please make sure you have access to the following apps: 

  • Custom Business Objects (F1712) 
  • Manage Substitution/Valdiation Rules – Service Documents (F7818) 

The above-mentioned apps are SAP pre-delivered under the following roles: 

  • SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC 
  • SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT 

What are Substitution Rules? 

Substitution rules signify which profit center, costing sheet, functional area, settlement profile or costing variant is applied. 

When you create a service transaction, the system uses the conditions that you have defined in the substitution rule to derive the available target field for the item. When you make any changes to the service transaction item, the system automatically redetermines the profit center, costing sheet, functional area, settlement profile, or costing variant using the conditions which were defined for the item. (Source: SAP Help) 

How and When to Use Substitution Rules? 

You create a substitution rule to determine the profit center based on the material (service product) and the plant. The system uses the plant-dependent product master to determine the profit center. If you change the product, the system redetermines the profit center. From the 2502 release on, you can derive profit centers in service contracts with new fields, such as Sold-to-Party, Ship-to-Party, or Bill-to-Party using the app Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents. 

Precondition: 

You use the precondition section to identify a field with a certain value to be used for substitution.  

Substitution: 

You use the substitution section to determine the field value to be substituted with a new field value. 

What is a lookup table? 

In the app Custom Business Objects, you create a new business object with a table, in which you can find both, the precondition value for the source field and the substitution value for the target field, which will receive the value found in the lookup table associated with the precondition field. 

How to Use the Lookup Table in the Substitution Tool 

Disclaimer: In this blog, for better readability, we will use the Sold-to Party field as an example. You can do the same process for the Ship-to Party or Bill-to Party fields. 

So, let’s get into it. 

To create a lookup table to use in the profit center derivation, you first have to create a new business object with a table with the desired fields in the Custom Business Objects app. 

In the Create Custom Business Objects in SAP S/4HANA Cloud tutorial group (requires partner/customer license) it shows how to create a custom business object and generate a user interface in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

The table has to contain the source field, in this case the Sold-to Party (SoldToParty) and the target field, which is the Profit Center (ProfitCenter). After adding the fields, you must check the “User Interface” checkbox and publish the business object to be able to use it in the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

After publishing the business object, you must add the tile, which you created by checking the “User Interface” checkbox, to your business catalog. To find out how to do that, see How to Add Custom Catalog Extensions | SAP Help Portal. 

You can now open the tile from your Fiori Launchpad and add values for the fields you added. 

After that, you can now use this lookup table in the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app. 

In the Manage Substitution/Validation Rules – Service Documents app, you create the substitution rule in the following way: 

Use the Precondition to specify the source field to be used for substitution. For this case, we are using the Sold-to Party. Make sure you set the Operator to “Is not empty”. 

After that, you specify the value and the target field to be used in the substitution. To do so, set the Target Field to Profit Center and use the Substitution Type “Table Lookup”. 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

To find the correct value to be substituted, you have to specify the data source and the condition, which you can do using the Source field. To do so, open the value help of the Source field and specify a Data Source and the Source Field. As the Data Source, select the lookup table you created earlier in the Custom Business Objects app. As the Source Field, select the Identifier of the Profit Center field that you used in the lookup table. 

You can now set the conditions that specify when the field will be substituted. To do so, in the Conditions field, select the Identifier of the Sold-to Party you used in the lookup table. As the Operator, you have to use “Equal to FieldFunction”. In the Value field, you then have to select the Identifier of the Sold-to Party (SoldToParty) field in the Service Document. 

You can check the “Overwrite” checkbox to make sure the value you determined for the profit center overwrites any previously filled-in value in the target field. 

After you have activated the rule, the system checks whether the Sold-to Party field filled in the Service Document equals the Sold-to Party field in the lookup table. If this is true, the corresponding profit center from the lookup table will be used in the Service Contract.  

This should save you some time during profit center determination, and if you want to look further, here are some resources from SAP Help: 

  • Profit Center Determination for Service Transactions 
  • Manage Substitution/Validation Rules - Service Documents 
  • Creating Custom Business Objects | SAP Help Portal 
  • Creating Extension Items and Transporting Them | SAP Help Portal 
  • Create Custom Business Objects in S/4HANA Cloud | SAP Tutorials

entry.title: Re: Explanation of warehouse process tasks
entry.content: Thanks a lot!

entry.title: Upcoming Webinar | Confidently Navigate the EU Pay Transparency Directive
entry.content:
It’s 2026 and one of the most sweeping pay equity legislations is about to go into effect – are you ready? As EU member states finalize new requirements to align with the EU Pay Transparency Directive, now is the perfect time to assess your current compensation and reporting practices so you can stay one step ahead.

To help you prepare for these changes, we’re hosting a webinar on February 25th at 1 PM CET - Ready on Day One: Confidently Navigate the EU Pay Transparency Directive. Leading experts will provide insight into what’s coming and how your organization can leverage SAP solutions to drive compliance. Here’s a quick summary of what we’ll be covering:

  • Timeline and key requirements of the EU Pay Transparency Directive
  • Best practices for enhancing pay transparency across the employee lifecycle
  • How SAP solutions can help you analyze, mitigate, and report on pay gaps

We look forward to seeing you there!

Register Here

entry.title: Re: Accelerate Insights with the new Quick Builder in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content: Very useful feature indeed !

entry.title: How to make a field mandatory or warning trigger for miro
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

 

We are using public cloud we wanted to make a field warning or error how do we proceed it in this scenario .

eg:-in miro in the headtab to make as warning message to enter ksef number :

entry.title: Re: Decision Logic Modernization in SAP Integrations: Using RPT-1 for Intelligent Routing Decisions
entry.content:
Are you augmenting the history data with the current selection as well? 

Definitely not a general use case but for the given use case, probably a good way to achieve this. 

Did you observe any mis-routed orders so far that actually delayed the delivery? That metric would help understand the accuracy of the solution as well.

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
Thank you for the reminder that the community belongs to everyone @qmacro 😃

To add a couple of comments here before anyone asks: 

  1. The "Recent Activity" feeds on the platform: hopefully in March we will deliver a change to the filters on the "Recent Activity" feeds on this platform. The filters "Most commented", "Most liked" and "Most viewed" will be changed so that we add a time constraint - that way making it easier to find recently published valuable content, ie. content that got likes, comments or views. 
  2. DSA compliance/external moderators: just to repeat again - we did not remove the external moderator role (as in external to the SAP Community team) because we wanted to. Due to DSA regulations, we need to always ensure we are compliant, which is why it is currently not possible for us to re-introduce the external moderator role as we used to know it. Having said that, we are continuously looking at how we can open things back up, and we are evaluating options.

entry.title: Re: What's new: SAP Best Practices for SAP Integrated Business Planning – 2602
entry.content: after 2602 upgrade IBP Analytics use is unable to save analytics. and even after save its showing the same data.

entry.title: Re: SAP Security Configuration Recommendations
entry.content:
Hello @antonio_steinhuser please find the relevant documentation below:

ECS Mandatory Security Configurations are documented here:

  • 3381209 - Information on mandatory Security Parameters & Hardening requirements for JAVA systems in SAP Enterprise Cloud Services - SAP for Me
  • 3250501 - Information on mandatory Security Parameters & Hardening requirements for ABAP systems in SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) - SAP for Me
  • 3480723 - Information on mandatory Security Parameters & Hardening requirements for SAP HANA databases in SAP Enterprise Cloud Services (ECS) - SAP for Me

SAP recommended configurations for on-premise / RISE are documented here:
https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/offerings-and-programs/support-services/sap-security-optimization-services-portfolio/Security_Baseline_Template_V2.zip

Best Regards,
Wihem

entry.title: Re: C_IEE2E Practical Exam Under Review → Attempt Closed...
entry.content: Dear Margit, thank you for the clarification and for confirming the availability of individual score reporting for recent system-based assessments. I appreciate the recredit of my previous attempt and will proceed with scheduling a second attempt for C_IEE2E shortly. It is helpful to know that the detailed scores will be visible under My Learning → Certification after completion. Kind regards Taofik

entry.title: Re: SAP Security Configuration Recommendations
entry.content:
Hi @briangonsales ,
Thank you for the notification. The document is being reviewed and will be re-published afterwards.
Best Regards
Wihem

Security_Baseline_Template_V2

entry.title: Re: Currency/valuation type 70 does not existMessage No....
entry.content: Please try to give as much information as possible when you create a discussion and expects some assistance from this forum.  The said error KM133 will be triggered from various transactions and it is not clear which transaction you were trying.  Check SAP notes 2613256, 2997338 & 3126387 which provides various solutions depending upon your issue.

entry.title: Ready on Day One: Confidently Navigate the EU Pay Transparency Directive
entry.content:
As EU member states rapidly begin adopting new pay transparency legislations, HR leaders must assess their current compensation and reporting practices to ensure compliance. In this session, leading experts will provide insight into what’s coming and how your organization can stay one step ahead with the right processes and technology in place.

Join us to discover:

  • Timeline and key requirements of the EU Pay Transparency Directive
  • Best practices for enhancing pay transparency across the employee lifecycle
  • How SAP solutions can help you analyze, mitigate, and report on pay gaps

Register now

entry.title: -306 - El nombre de recurso especificado no se encuentra en el archivo de imagen.
entry.content:
Hola,  tengo el siguiente problema, tras una restauracion de la mv donde tengo montado sap, los desarrollos que tenemos conectados por di api nos arrojan el siguiente error:
-306 - El nombre de recurso especificado no se encuentra en el archivo de imagen.
tambien tenemos problemas usando el data transfer de sap  nos arroja el mismo error, creo que puede ser alguna configuracion del di api que hay que revisar  pero no se que configuracion habria que cambiar.

entry.title: Re: Public Cloud DRC Turkey Sales List Error while checki...
entry.content:
1. Confirm that the Statutory Reports app configuration is complete (especially storage category).
  2. Check if the user has the correct business role.
  3. Run the report for a period with known invoices to rule out “no content” errors.
  4. If the error persists, raise an SAP Incident

entry.title: Support of a Compact Display for Tables (Live BW)
entry.content:
Introduction

As part of our continuous innovation for Live BW connectivity, we are introducing Compact Display for Tables with the 2026 Q1 QRC release.

Compact Display is designed to simplify complex table structures by consolidating multiple dimensions into a single, structured hierarchy within a row or column.

The goal is to enhance readability, optimize space usage, and improve analytical clarity, especially in enterprise scenarios with deep dimensional breakdowns.

For customers familiar with the “Universal Display Hierarchy” in SAP BEx Query Designer, Compact Display delivers a similar conceptual benefit in SAC’s Optimized Experience, while being purpose-built for SAC architecture and modern UX standards.

 

What is the Feature “Compact Display”?

Compact Display allows you to group multiple dimensions on a row or column axis into a single structured display, enabling expandable nodes instead of separate stacked dimensions.

What it does

When activated on an axis:

  • All dimensions bound to that axis are grouped into one single row or column structure
  • The structure follows the top-to-bottom order of entities bound to the axis
  • Users can expand or collapse nodes interactively
  • The header displays the names of the compacted Dimensions/Measures
  • Display Attributes are not compacted and remain as separate rows/columns

What it does not do

  • If the axis contains only measures, Compact Display:
    • Is unchecked
    • Is disabled
    • Displays the tooltip:
      "You can't apply compact display because this axis lacks dimensions or contains only measures."
  • If no dimensions or measures are bound to the axis, the option is disabled.
  • Compact Display does nothing when only measures exist on the axis.

Why it matters

In real-life enterprise reporting scenarios, tables can quickly become:

  • Wide and hard to read
  • Vertically heavy
  • Difficult to navigate

Compact Display helps you to:

  • Improve density without losing structure
  • Enhance hierarchical readability
  • Provide a cleaner user experience

 

How to Use It

Compact Display is available in the Optimized Experience. If not yet enabled, you must opt-in to the “New Table Builder Experience”.

Step-by-Step Activation

  1. Open your story
  2. Select your Table
  3. In the Builder Panel, locate the Row or Column axis
  4. Click the “…” menu
  5. Select Compact Display

Behavior logic

Scenario

Compact Display Option

No real dimension on axis (only measures or empty)

Disabled, unchecked, tooltip shown

At least one dimension added

Enabled; any new dimension or measure added to the axis will be added to the Compact Display

Last real dimension removed

Disabled again, unchecked, tooltip shown

 

See It in Action

 

Share Your Feedback

Compact Display is an enhancement for Live BW scenarios, and your feedback is essential.

It directly helps us improve both the product and its documentation.

Please share your thoughts and experience through:

  • SAP Community discussions
  • Customer Influence channels

Your input is highly appreciated and will actively influence future enhancements to SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: RITA - Tax Ctry/Reg in standard reports
entry.content:
Hi, 

We've activated RITA for our customer with a company code in NL and Foreign Plant in BE running Public cloud. Now we'r testing and trying to find the easiest way of analyzing the VAT-postings that will happen. We know that we can run Create Advance Return on Sales/Purchase tax and filter on the Tax Country and only find the posting belonging to one tax registration number, are we missing anything? Is there any other way report we can extract? 

For analyzing purposes we've been trying to see if it's possible to add the dimension Tax Country/Region into reports such as for example Display Line Items in General Ledger, but we cant find what we'r looking for. Is it usually only customs fields that can be added into standards reports and not SAP standard fields?

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Re: Using the EXTENSIONIN parameter of BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CR...
entry.content:
What exactly did you mean when you mentioned that the function module documentation is incorrect?

  • For an example of filling EXTENSIONIN in a similar BAPI, look at FM MASTERIDOC_CREATE_SMD_BAMMAST (look  for 'Fill extension segment')

entry.title: SAP CodeJam em Joinville! 🚀
entry.content:
O SAP CodeJam desembarca em Joinville trazendo uma experiência imersiva para quem quer colocar a mão na massa com inteligência artificial dentro do universo SAP.

Durante o encontro, os participantes irão explorar na prática:
💡Generative AI Hub
🔎RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)

📍Ágora Tech Park
📅13 de março
⏰9h às 15h

O treinamento será conduzido por Jessica De Marchi @jessicademarchi  trazendo conhecimento técnico aplicado, troca com a comunidade e muito desenvolvimento hands-on.

Agradecimento especial aos patrocinadores oficiais
Este evento acontece com o apoio de:
Axe Tecnologia, CHPro, Coinov, CVA, Escotta, MakeValue e Stefanini.
O suporte de cada um é fundamental para fortalecer a comunidade SAP na região e tornar encontros como este possíveis.

Participação gratuita!
Garanta sua vaga realizando a inscrição pela SAP Community:
🔗 https://www.sympla.com.br/evento/sap-codejam-joinville-2026/3314100

As vagas são limitadas, então vale correr para garantir seu lugar! 🚀

#sapcodejam #SAP #SITJLLE2026 #comunidadeSAP

entry.title: Header Billing Plan net value is 0
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I am creating a sales order in Fiori app with more than 20+ line items. All items are services using item category CTAD (milestone billing plan).

In the Fiori app, the billing plan can only be maintained at item level. Since maintaining it separately for each item is time-consuming, I save the sales order first then go to "Change Sales Orders" GUI application to maintain the billing plan at header level instead.

However, when I try to set up the billing plan at header level in the GUI app, the net value field is zero.

The sales order itself is complete and has a net value, but I am unable to invoice it because the billing plan value remains zero.

Could this issue be caused by creating the sales order in the Fiori app (where header-level billing plan is not available) and then attempting to apply a header-level billing plan in the GUI application?

What's the solution in this case?

Setting up a billing plan 20 times is not a solution.

Why isn't there header level billing plan in Fiori app?

Apprecite your help.

Best regards,

Zsombor

entry.title: Re: Treasury Related Learning Journey & Certifications ar...
entry.content:
Hi,

Just a quick update on my SAP Treasury path. It turns out the certification world has changed quite a bit since my first one in 2013!

Back then, SAP handed out actual physical paper certificates (which I still have!). While my paper doesn't have an expiration date—and technically makes me a "classic"—it belongs to a different era of Certification 

As of January 2026, SAP has ditched the old-school multiple-choice exams. The new standard is focused on S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Currently, the specific Treasury certification is in a bit of a "loading" phase (likely being updated for the new year), so it’s not immediately available for booking.

I’ll be diving into the latest learning journeys in the meantime so that when the new digital badge drops, I’m ready to trade in my 2013 "relic" for the modern version!

entry.title: PP/DS – When are Max Earliness and Max Lateness evaluated in Dynamic Pegging
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have maintained Max Earliness and Max Lateness values (48 hours) at the material level in PP/DS.

To validate our configuration and perform internal debugging of a custom enhancement, we would like clarification on the standard system behavior.

In which standard transaction or process step are Max Earliness and Max Lateness evaluated (for example, /SAPAPO/RRP3 Product View, heuristic run, background scheduling, etc.)?

During which standard scheduling phase does the system read and apply these values?

Is there any standard documentation that explains the processing sequence for this logic in dynamic pegging?

This will help us verify whether our custom logic is aligned with the standard PP/DS scheduling and dynamic pegging process.

Thanks,
Abinayashree

entry.title: Re: Temporary Login Delay after ABAP Trial 2602 Upgrade
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We’re pleased to inform you that the login delay issue affecting new ABAP Trial service instances has been successfully resolved.

Users can now create new ABAP Trial instances and log in immediately after provisioning completes, without experiencing any delays.

We appreciate your patience and understanding while our team worked to resolve this issue.

Best regards,
Paul

entry.title: Re: Employee Central Dependent Workflow optimisation
entry.content:
Hi @Sindhu_A ,

Please refer - https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2343618 

Please vote for https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/346815 

Regards,

VR

entry.title: Re: Currency/valuation type 70 does not existMessage No....
entry.content: Ensure that the currencies listed in Transaction Code FINSC_LEDGER are correct. The currencies specified in Transaction Code OMX2 must be a subset of the FI currency types.

entry.title: Re: Employee Central Dependent Workflow optimisation
entry.content:
Hi @Sindhu_A ,

Please refer - https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2343618

Please vote - https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/346815 

Regards,

VR

entry.title: SAP SAC how to populate a measure value to all line items
entry.content:
In SAC, I need to populate the Restricted measure value to all line items in the Story. Measure 1 and 2 are Restricted Measures. Measure 1 = category 'XX'  and 'Sales', measure 2 = Product Line 'ALL'' and 'Gross'. How can I populate ?? with the same value 500 for all the line items. This should happen even if I filter out Product 'OTHER'. from the Table. Data actions is not an option in my case.

ProductMeasure 1Measure 2Calc Measure  
OTHER2050020/500 
AA30??30/500 
BB40??40/500 
CC20??20/500

entry.title: if WBS code length limit is 25digit, why the length of project ID in migration template is 80?
entry.content:
Dear all,

Far as I know, in S/4 Hana cloud, the length limit of Project ID is 24digit, but I found the length of project id length in migration template is 80. just wandering if I import legacy projects with ID more than 24digit, and less then 80, what will happen ? 

B,R

Gary

entry.title: SAP S4Hana Public Cloud - Custom CDS View - Need Credit Limit Field for AR Reporting
entry.content: Hello. I am looking to find a field for Custom AR Reporting purposes. The field I am looking for is. “Credit limit” as it exists in the “Manage Credit Accounts” App. Is there a good place for me to locate this field?

entry.title: EWM Component staging not initiated
entry.content:
Hello experts.

We are implementing EWM in public cloud. In our system we stage using material independent control cycle using pick part strategy as shown:

Now, what we do is, after release of production orders, we go to functions -> WM Material Staging -> Execute. When we do that we see that the documents generated in the process posting change item app as shown below:

So our customizing system is working fine. Now we are in test system and what we are noticing is when we execute the staging, the quantity is not requested as shown in the simulation below:

In the log I see the message: Message 118 - Batch verification no required, although possible in WM (pick parts):

We have never seen this message in customizing system. Also one more thing I have noticed that in the component view 'Change Process Type' field is marked as 1 (Manually Changed):

In customizing system I have seen this field is blank.

Could you please help me understand what is the root cause of this issue? Why am I not able to execute the staging process? Any help/insight will be highly appreciated.

 

Thank you

Snigdhodeep

entry.title: Re: Learning Admin > Generate Direct Link > Current Regis...
entry.content:
@sourabhkumar0 wrote:

Hi @VeljkoRoskar The reported issue may be due to the fact that 'Current Registrations', 'Surveys', 'Recommended Items', and 'Library Simple Search' are currently unsupported through Direct Link in the New Learning Experience. Reference KBA - 3511497 - New Learning Experience: New Home Page 


Thanks. However, from overall system design perspective, features that do not work should not be available as options. So, will this be fixed in 2026 or should I submit a customer influence to remove from "Generate Direct Link" the 'Current Registrations', 'Surveys', 'Recommended Items', and 'Library Simple Search' options?

entry.title: Re: Journal entry - Add standard fields in Substitution rules
entry.content:
Hey @Cora_Phelan ,

is this also relevant for the onPremise that no further standard fields could be added to the target substitution fields in the Fiori App?

entry.title: Re: Error in Year Closing due to AUCs with Net Book Value...
entry.content: After updating the depreciation area configurations to “All Values Allowed” and enabling the “Negative Values Allowed” checkbox for both AUCs, the issue has been resolved. Many thanks to Nag_7589 for the kind guidance and support.

entry.title: Re: Update before current payroll period is not allowed
entry.content:
Hello,

please check the content of the attached note:

3603166 - Update before current payroll period is not allowed - Message No. HRSFEC_SERVICES292 - SAP for Me

in particular, the following sentence:

Make sure that no customer BADi  (e.g. HRSFEC_B_CE_CHANGE_IT0009) is changing/creating/deleting IT0009 start date impacting on having IT0009 changes prior to the "delimit_start_date"

maybe it is the explanation of your issue.

Best Regards

Giorgio

entry.title: Re: How-to-Guide SAP Fiori Relevance and Readiness Analysis
entry.content:
Hi @kirankola : Getting below notification while accessing the link  Simplified Fiori Software Provisioning with Maintenance Planner. 

Regards,

Bharanidharan S P M

entry.title: Re: Using the EXTENSIONIN parameter of BAPI_BILLINGDOC_CR...
entry.content:
Hi,
This is the example copied directly from the documentation - see line 18:

DATA: lo_bapi_mapping          TYPE REF TO if_cfd_bapi_mapping,
       lr_header_bapi_ext       TYPE REF TO bape_sdbillingdoc,
       EXTENSIONIN              TYPE bapiparextab,
       lv_key TYPE sdbillingdoc_key,
       lv_vgbel type vgbel,
       lv_vgpos type vgpos.

*data initialization
CREATE DATA lr_header_bapi_ext.
lo_bapi_mapping = cl_cfd_bapi_mapping=>get_instance( ).

*Fill the custom fields with values
*In case you want to create a billing document with reference to 900000001 and item 10:
lv_vgbel = '90000001'.
lv_vgpos = '10'.
CONCATENATE lv_vgbel lv_vgpos into lv_key.
lr_header_bapi_ext->key = lv_key.
lr_header_bapi_ext->data- YY1_CUTEST_BDH = 'testvalueXX'.

*map the custom fields to the EXTENSIONIN
lo_bapi_mapping->map_to_bapiparex_single(
EXPORTING
   ir_source_structure = lr_header_bapi_ext
   CHANGING
      ct_bapiparex = EXTENSIONIN )

The structure value needs to be BAPE_SDBILLINGDOC in order for it to be identified as containing data (in the BAPI).

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Manage My Timesheet (F1823) – Why is it possible to r...
entry.content:
It is currently standard behavior that time entries can still be entered in a period where the CO period is closed.

There is an existing Improvement Request Lock Time Recording in closed periods which requests that such time entries be prevented. I would encourage you to vote on this.

In the meantime you can test whether BAdI HCM_CATS_V1_TS_VALIDATION can be coded to meet your requirements as per KBA 3616984.

entry.title: Re: XAPI Support for SF Learning is coming - what will be...
entry.content: Now planned for Q2 2026 per roadmap link.

entry.title: Re: Introduce profit center postings for subsequent activ...
entry.content:
Hi Vaibhav,

Thanks for the response.

In our current design, Profit Center derivation for Sales Order line items and WBS elements is from the material master (MARC table). We intend to replicate this derivation logic within the FI substitution exit to ensure uniform Profit Center assignment between SD-relevant items and WBS elements across all integration points.

Please confirm whether the document splitting operates exclusively on the accounting document data (both stored and runtime line items generated during posting), or if the document splitting logic also evaluates logistics-origin fields from SD/MM objects (e.g., Sales Orders, Deliveries, Material Master, WBS).

 

Thanks,

Ravi

entry.title: Comp Ratio Story Report from Content Store
entry.content:
Trying to use the comp ratio report from Content store, unfortunately it does not work because we do use/store metadata in Geozone and legal entity tables referencing from Pay Grade. After removing these tables, the output shows multiple payranges in different currencies. 

How do I filter the employee specific pay range and avoid multiple currency pay ranges.

entry.title: GL Account not available as Preconditition for Market Segment Substitution
entry.content:
In S4HC Public, in the app Manage Substitution and Validation Rules (Market Segment Business context), we need to create a substitution whose precondition is a given GL Account. Unfortunately, this field is not available in the list (see capture 1 attached).

However, the GL account is available for the substitutions done through the custom logic BADI FINS_MARKET_SEGMENT_SUB (Market Segment Extension Fields Substitution).

 
 
Is it a mistake and SAP forgot to include GL account to be used in the app Manage Substitution?
We don't want to create custom field ZZ_glaccount and populate it using the before mentioned custom logic, as proposed for other issues as "clean core solution". Do we need to raise a note/incidence to SAP?
 
Thanks. Regards,

entry.title: Not able to assign Mitigation Control to the Monitor of same Control
entry.content: We need to assign an MC for a User who is also the Monitor for this MC . How can we accomplish this ?

entry.title: Re: if WBS code length limit is 25digit, why the length o...
entry.content:
Hello @garyding ,

Good question!!. Please read this KBA by SAP .  The 80-character limit in the template exists only to ensure your legacy data can be "staged" without loss; it does not override the business logic of the target S/4HANA environment. 

There is one more sheet in the standard template which has the acceptable character limit. 

Regards,

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Re: Using CALM Features to manage C4C & IBP Transports
entry.content:
Hi, GrahamH,
You can find a list of Supported Solutions at https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-alm/setup-administration/supported-solutions.

Seems neither C4C (SAP Cloud for Customer) nor IBP (SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain) work with Change and Deployment Management.

Cloud ALM has no input on the services/systems that engage with the various functions in Cloud ALM -> each source decides when/if they want to work with Cloud ALM's functionality and they work with our development team to make that a possibility.  So, if you would like to see these systems work with Cloud ALM, you should work with them to let them know of your need for such support.  (If they have an Influence Campaign, similar to the ones in Cloud ALM, you can submit your idea there.)

I hope this is helpful to you.

Best regards,
Paula

entry.title: Re: EWM Component staging not initiated
entry.content:
• Have you checked whether the EWM material master data has been maintained?
  • Also, please verify that the storage location (SLOC) is extended to EWM.
  • Are you using Advanced Production Integration?

entry.title: Re: Joule Is Not a Chatbot: How AI Agents on SAP BTP Help North American Utilities Reduce Risk
entry.content:
I’m curious to hear from others working in Utilities or high‑volume billing environments. Where do you see the biggest opportunity for AI agents on SAP BTP to reduce operational friction?

Is it:

  • exception reduction in billing
  • field operations and work management
  • outage/customer communications
  • credit & collections
  • or something entirely different based on your experience

Would love to hear real‑world perspectives from the community. The more examples we share, the easier it becomes to separate practical value from hype.

entry.title: Re: How to add F4 in Standard Fiori Apps for extended SAP Standard fields
entry.content:
Hello Naina,

I have followed your blog to enhance the Odata service, In my Case, I have an custom association (for Search Help)  and  a Custom field to be displayed in the Standard App.

Note:  Enabling Searching help is important as the Business User wants to filter the custom column displayed.

  1. Added the field and association  through "Extend view" 
  2. I have extended the Standard Odata service with custom with Overwrite option enabled.
  3. Created Association and Associationset with same Custom Association name.
  4. Added the merged SADL XML in DPC_EXT and MPC_EXT  with UUID and  latest time as per the blog 

I  get a  cache issue and cant get past this error, even though i have used all cache clear Tcodes. 
Once the Cache issue is fixed, I never get that issue any more.

Analysis:

Initially, there is a cache issue  between Cache Table /IWBEP/I_MGW_CAC and the Last modified BADI Fall Back class as a result it loops through the  ls_model-header-extended_model at it has entry of the Standard Service instead of Custom Service as a result it does not know the Custom association and dumps.

I had the same issue, in my Sandbox, Dev, QA .. some how after numerous times running the cache clear for 30 times, I got it fixed in Sandbox, Dev. But not in QA.

How do I  fix it ?

entry.title: How to get the security related incidents alert from BTP Applications
entry.content:
Team,

We need to know how to get the alerts from BTP applications if security related incidents happens.

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to get the security related incidents alert from...
entry.content:
Please take a look into the SAP Alert Notification Service for SAP BTP:

https://help.sap.com/docs/alert-notification/sap-alert-notification-for-sap-btp/initial-setup

entry.title: Is it possible to dynamically update FTE/Contingent workers count in Company Org Chart?
entry.content: Is it possible to show only the count of FTE in company Org Chart  if I updated the setting to exclude contingent workers?

entry.title: Re: EWM Component staging not initiated
entry.content:
Hi!
Thanks for the inputs, here are the comments:

  • Have you checked whether the EWM material master data has been maintained? Yes. They are maintained completely.
  • Also, please verify that the storage location (SLOC) is extended to EWM. It is. We are performing other EWM transactions in this Sloc.
  • Are you using Advanced Production Integration? Note sure I understood this question. Can you please elaborate?

 

Thank you

Snigdhodeep

entry.title: Re: S4HC 2602 output management: maintain email address f...
entry.content: Hi Matthias, without making any changes, the system derives the desired email address now. I believe it was a temporary bug. Thanks again for your insights!

entry.title: Re: Configuration of Depreciation Key for Italy
entry.content: can you please share document

entry.title: Custom Business Object OData call failing with SYSTEM_NO_ROLL error when fetching x-csrf-token
entry.content:
We have a Custom Business Object (CBO) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that works correctly in one system but fails in another after transport.

When testing the OData service via Postman, the GET request used to fetch the x-csrf-token returns a SYSTEM_NO_ROLL runtime error. The same logic works in a system with smaller data volume.

The short dump shows:

  • Runtime Error: SYSTEM_NO_ROLL
  • Category: Resource bottleneck
  • Error location: /IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_WRITER_XML
  • Message: Memory request could not be completed

The issue initially appeared to be related to CSRF token retrieval, but further analysis suggests the error occurs during response serialization.

What is the root cause of this behavior and what is the recommended solution?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Custom Business Object OData call failing with SYSTEM...
entry.content:
This behavior is typically caused by unfiltered OData API calls retrieving very large datasets, which exhaust available session memory during response serialization.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, if an API such as:

/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder

(or similar standard APIs like Billing, Delivery, etc.) is called without any $filter or $top parameter, the system attempts to retrieve and serialize all records in the corresponding entity set.

In systems with high transactional data volume (e.g., hundreds of thousands or millions of records), this can lead to:

  • SYSTEM_NO_ROLL
  • TSV_TNEW_PAGE_ALLOC_FAILED
  • STRING_SIZE_TOO_LARGE
  • HTTP 500 Internal Server Error

The dump occurs during OData response generation (/IWCOR/CL_DS_EP_WRITER_XML) because the system runs out of memory while building the response payload.

How to Resolve

Always restrict OData API calls using query parameters such as $top or $filter.

Example 1 – Limit result size

GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder?$top=1000

Example 2 – Retrieve a specific document

GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder?$filter=SalesOrder eq '123456'

Example 3 – Combine filter and limit

GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/A_SalesOrder?$filter=SalesOrganization eq '1000'&$top=500

For large-scale data extraction, implement pagination using $skiptoken or iterative $top calls instead of retrieving the full dataset in one request.

Important Notes

  • This is expected behavior when large unfiltered datasets are requested.
  • No data deletion is required.
  • The solution is to optimize API usage with proper filtering and limits.

This behavior is also described in SAP Knowledge Base Articles addressing large-volume OData requests and SYSTEM_NO_ROLL runtime errors. 3509792 - API_BILLING_DOCUMENT_SRV returning runtime error 'SYSTEM_NO_ROLL' - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: The 2025 Developer Insights Survey: The Report
entry.content:
@rileyrainey I've not found a post announcing 2026 survey, so adding the comments about it here. 

1. Like others, I'm concerned about the time it took to publish the survey results. Hopefully, we'll see the results for 2026 survey before the year ends.

2. Is it possible to add some kind of progress indicator to the survey? Or warn people how long would it take? Or better yet, reduce number of questions? I was already getting fatigued by super detailed GenAI related questions, then got hit by a huge wall of text scale type question and just noped out of it. And then there were what felt like 99 more questions after that. I bet many people will also get tired and you won't get good quality answers. 

3. Why is there this question every year: "For your recent experience developing an integration or extension, how would you describe your overall satisfaction with SAP?" What exactly do you expect people to answer? What metrics does it drive? "I had a problem with this BAdI and now I don't feel good about SAP?" Then what? ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

4. I could not enter "Jules" in "Other" field, it raised an error that I'm entering sensitive personal information. Uh...  It's a Google product. Might want to check the validation there.

And just a minor thing: no option to "specify" in this question format. 

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Utilising the Define Eligibility for Onboarding Initiation Rule
entry.content:
Hi, Following Nathan's guidance in this blog I now have this working for a client.  Just wanted to call out somethings we tried to do differently which didn't work (to save anyone time also setting this up)

1) Instead of using a custom field on the application template we tried to use the standard source field and select Internal Hire, this just didnt work.

 

2) Looked into getting it to work with a field on the offer, the parameters on the eligibility rule do not allow for this.

Thanks so much to Nathan for such good guidance.

entry.title: Re: Requirement Date in Subcontracting Component SAP S4 H...
entry.content:
Hello @Chris1973,

Thank you so much for your time and comments.

You've been a great help.

🙂

entry.title: Re: I Asked 50 SAP Professionals What Scares Them About AI. Here's What They Said.
entry.content:
@neel247 

Thanks for sharing. This makes me think. 

My concern about AI is this: people say "human in the loop", assuming that humans can make the right judgment on AI's outputs. Today, it may be true. But what about 5 years from now? AI will be there right after they enter the business world. Will they have the chance to understand what AI is doing behind the scenes? They may simply follow whatever AI tells them. Then, who can be the "human in the loop"?

entry.title: Re: Improving the SAP Community together, one comment at a time
entry.content:
Unfortunately, I have to disagree with you here, DJ.
Yes, at one point it was the community’s job to correct wrong information and start discussions to clarify and improve unclear content. But that’s not really the case anymore.
Bad actors show up with a swarm of LLM drones, and SAP gives the moderators muskets. And then the rest is supposed to be handled by the community?
The AI tags that label a post as AI-generated aren’t a bad idea in principle. But can I filter out all posts using them? Unfortunately, no. Is the tag always used correctly? Obviously not.
I’m not a native speaker and I’m not a great writer, so AI helps me with wording and translation, because of that, all my posts get that tag. So the tag doesn’t help much either.
It’s an unfair fight and honestly, it’s already lost. And I’ve given up.
When I reported something, I sometimes end up having to argue why I think it should be removed. And obvious product advertising was not removed.
And with the sheer number of completely AI-generated posts, the community simply doesn’t have the time or capacity to correct them. A post can be generated in seconds, whether it’s true or not. Reading and properly correcting a post takes much longer.
Personally, I’d set a much higher quality bar but I understand why that’s difficult for several reasons: the DSA issues Anne mentioned, limited resources in the moderator team, or simply a strategic decision not to invest more money into the SAP Community.
I was optimistic for a long time, but over the last year nothing has happened that makes me feel more positive.

That makes me genuinely sad, because I’ve learned a lot from this community and I’d like to give something back. But all the hurdles put in our way make it hard, frustrating, and almost impossible. Especially if you remember how it was before the last platform migration.
What makes it even worse is that the only real alternative in terms of reach and networking is LinkedIn. And the AI slop there is even worse: the few good posts get drowned in low-quality LLM infographics and outdated ABAP code. 

I really want to feel more optimistic but I don’t see any progress in that direction for more quality.

entry.title: Re: SAP BDC TDD - Create Data Product - Object Store requ...
entry.content: And what is "awkward" is this requirement of having at least 1TB of Object Store here. Since this is a TDD system, having say 10-100x less as a minimum would be quite OK for learning and testing. Then it would be possible to drive the minimum memory requirement accordingly...

entry.title: Confirmation of Scope Item 5XU activation and Basic Statutory Reporting
entry.content:
1)The Tax Procedure 0TXIN exists in our Quality System. Does this indicate that Scope Item 5XU is fully active?

2. Availability of Basic Statutory Reports
The "Basic Compliance Reporting" functionalities included in the standard license. is it possible to generate the following minimum required tax reports using the "Run Statutory Reports" (App ID: F1515) app?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Confirmation of Scope Item 5XU activation and Basic S...
entry.content:
Dear all,

 

Please find the below answer,

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, no GST components are maintained under 0TXIN. GST works under SAP-delivered India tax configuration, not the legacy 0TXIN procedure.

Under India GST in Public Cloud, the following components are delivered within procedure 0TXIN. Example below,

GST Tax Components (Condition Types inside 0TXIN)

SAP delivers GST-relevant condition types such as:

CGST – Central GST

SGST – State GST

IGST – Integrated GST

UTGST – Union Territory GST

These are embedded in 0TXIN and activated via tax codes.

Yes, it is possible to generate the following minimum required tax reports using the run statutory reports app. The Document and Reporting Compliance (5XU) scope item fulfills all real-time and statutory reporting requirements, including end-to-end processing of transaction controls, digital link, and online submission requirements. Enabling visibility of advanced features of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance.

 

Best regards,

Atanu

SAP Support - S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Re: Deficit of Stck w.vend.unr./rev X PC XXX IN PO Subcon...
entry.content:
Hello @Chris1973,

Thank you so much for your time and comments.
It's clear now.
🙂

entry.title: Hi experts, I have a below scenario about posting unplanned scrap quantity in header level of produ
entry.content:
Hi experts,

 

I have a below scenario about posting unplanned scrap quantity in header level of production order.

 

If we have a production order for 10 pcs and while producing the assembly (GR is not posted, but only GI is posted) before the final operation is performed the assembly is found to be defective and it has to be scrapped. So we do a partial confirmation for with scrap quantity as 1 pc and this scrap quantity gets posted in operations level of production order .

 

But in production order header still the order quantity will be 10 pcs and delivered quantity is 0. Scrap portion and shortage deviation is 0.

 

Now MRP considers that still order has 10 pcs of order quantity, but in reality the MES system has only 9 more pieced to be produced and this will prevent MRP in creating new planned orders .

We always do partial confirmation for each parts and in case of any repair or rework or scrapping the production takes in months to take a decision and this is causing issues in planning

I am thinking if we can use BAPI_PRODORD_CHANGE by passing the scrap quantity in order header level, by doing so my order quantity will be 9 pcs and it will generate the dependent requirements accordingly.

 

I would like to know if anyone else has faced this issue and can we update the unplanned scrap quantity in production order header level?

entry.title: Re: Standard Currency Translation always yields 1:1 result
entry.content: Nvm, figured it out - once I maintained rate records for a base curr and rate type in the model it worked.
